parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: The lower time boundary represented as milliseconds since epoch.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: The upper time boundary represented as milliseconds since epoch.

parameters.name: resolution
parameters.description: The segment duration represented as milliseconds. Supported values are 1 minute (60000), 5 minutes (300000), 15 minutes (900000), 1 hour (3600000), 1 day (86400000) and 1 week (604800000).

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Segments can be offset using this parameter. Expressed in milliseconds. Can only be used if the resolution is greater than 1 hour. Value must be in whole hours, less than the provided resolution, and less than 24 hours.

parameters.name: base_asset_type
parameters.description: The type for the base asset. Either `native`, `credit_alphanum4`, or `credit_alphanum12`.

parameters.name: base_asset_issuer
parameters.description: The Stellar address of the base assetâ€™s issuer. Required if the `base_asset_type` is not `native`.

parameters.name: base_asset_code
parameters.description: The code for the base asset. Required if the `base_asset_type` is not `native`.

parameters.name: counter_asset_type
parameters.description: The type for the counter asset. Either `native`, `credit_alphanum4`, or `credit_alphanum12`.

parameters.name: counter_asset_issuer
parameters.description: The Stellar address of the counter assetâ€™s issuer. Required if the `counter_asset_type` is not `native`.

parameters.name: counter_asset_code
parameters.description: The code for the counter asset. Required if the `counter_asset_type` is not `native`.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: A designation of the order in which records should appear. Options include `asc` (ascending) or `desc` (descending). If this argument isnâ€™t set, it defaults to `asc`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of records returned. The limit can range from 1 to 200 - an upper limit that is hardcoded in Horizon for performance reasons. If this argument isnâ€™t designated, it defaults to 10.
